Examples

  • Gallery

line

The line mark turns an ordered, grouped point set into one polyline. It is an AGGREGATE mark: its N input rows collapse to a single SVG <path> per series (an array_agg over the points in SQL), so a table of (x, y) becomes one connected line. Points are drawn in the order of the order channel, which defaults to x. A stroke colour channel splits the input into one path per distinct value — one line per series. line is just path with a conventional name; the two marks are identical.

// N rows aggregate to ONE path per species, ordered by x (year). The series is modelled
// inline — a group-by averaging body mass per (species, year), visible in the SQL tab.
const series = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "year"], { mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.line(series, {
  x: linear("year"),
  y: linear("mass"),
  stroke: ordinal("species"),
  strokeWidth: 2,
}).axis("x").axis("y");
return v;

Three lines, one per species, each connecting its yearly average mass in year order.

Options

line is a stroked aggregate mark: it binds stroke, never fill (an unfilled path is a stroke, not a filled blob — drawPath defaults fill to "none"). Aggregation is per series: distinct stroke values partition the rows, and within each partition the points sort by order.

Channel Type Default Meaning
x positional-x — point x position (scale binding or pixel literal)
y positional-y — point y position
order number x the SEQUENCE column — the points sort by this before aggregating
stroke color — line colour; distinct values split into one path per series
strokeWidth pixel 1 line thickness (px)
strokeDasharray text — SVG dash pattern, e.g. "4 2"
strokeOpacity number 1 stroke alpha
opacity number 1 whole-mark alpha
title text — per-path <title> hover tooltip

The order channel takes an EXPRESSION, not a scale: use field("t") (aka col("t")) to name a raw column. A bare string is a literal, so order: "t" is wrong — always wrap the column.

Examples

A basic single line — no series split, ordered by x:

const yearly = db.table("penguins").groupby(["year"], { mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const v = canvas(480, 320);
v.line(yearly, { x: linear("year"), y: linear("mass"), stroke: "#4e79a7" })
 .axis("x").axis("y");
return v;

Multi-series by colour — one line per species, with a legend:

const series = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "year"], { mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
const p = v.line(series, {
  x: linear("year"),
  y: linear("mass"),
  stroke: ordinal("species"),
  strokeWidth: 2,
});
p.axis("x").axis("y").legend("stroke", { placement: "right" });
return v;

Time-ordered by an explicit order channel — the line follows time, not x, so it can double back on x:

const track = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "year"],
  { bill: avg("bill_length_mm"), mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.line(track, {
  x: linear("bill"),
  y: linear("mass"),
  order: field("year"),          // sequence by TIME — bill is not monotone
  stroke: ordinal("species"),
  strokeWidth: 2,
}).axis("x").axis("y");
return v;

Dashed styling and per-mark opacity:

const yearly = db.table("penguins").groupby(["year"], { mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const v = canvas(480, 320);
v.line(yearly, {
  x: linear("year"), y: linear("mass"),
  stroke: "#e15759", strokeWidth: 2, strokeDasharray: "6 3", strokeOpacity: 0.8,
}).axis("x").axis("y");
return v;

Layered over dots — draw the raw points and the connecting line over the same shared scales by reusing deferred scale objects:

const series = db.table("penguins").groupby(["species", "year"], { mass: avg("body_mass_g") });
const x = linear(), y = linear();
const v = canvas(640, 460);
v.line(series, { x: x("year"), y: y("mass"), stroke: ordinal("species"), strokeWidth: 2 });
v.dot(series, { x: x("year"), y: y("mass"), fill: ordinal("species"), r: 4 })
 .axis("x").axis("y");
return v;

See also

  • path — the aggregate primitive; line is path under another name
  • area — the filled aggregate: a band between (x, y) and a baseline
  • connect — the pairwise sibling: N points → N−1 styleable segments
  • rule · link — single-segment marks over paired columns
